title: First Click Breakthrough — 3 Pages Selected for Emergency Fix
selection_criteria: real pages, indexed, impressions > 0, position 5-30, clear user intent, fixable quickly

page_1.why_no_click: Position 10.9 — near bottom of page 1. Snippet may be giving away the payment amount ($405.53) so users don't need to click. Title is descriptive not curiosity-driven. Calculator CTA may not be prominent enough.
page_1.competitor_weakness: Competitors give answer in snippet too — opportunity to differentiate with 'See YOUR exact payment with amortization schedule' hook
fix_plan: Improve title: from descriptive to curiosity hook (e.g. '$20,000 Loan at 8%: See YOUR Exact Monthly Payment & Full Schedule')
Add stronger calculator CTA above the fold
Ensure the answer ($405.53) is visible but requires click for full schedule
Add FAQ schema with 'What is the monthly payment for a $20,000 loan at 8%?'
Add 2-3 internal links from this page to loan-calculator (money page)
page_1.expected_effect: CTR improvement from 0% to 1-3% at position 10.9 = 1-4 clicks/week

page_2.why_no_click: Only 2 impressions despite position 4.0 — the query is very low volume OR position 4 is for an ultra-specific query. Page may not match broader 'rent vs buy' queries.
page_2.competitor_weakness: Most rent vs buy pages are generic — QFINHUB can capture specific-income scenarios
fix_plan: Check if page title matches the actual query driving position 4
Expand page to cover 'rent vs buy calculator' queries (not just 100k income)
Add a rent-vs-buy calculator CTA prominently
Add FAQ: 'Is it better to rent or buy on a $100k salary?'
Add internal links to mortgage affordability calculator
page_2.expected_effect: If query coverage expands, impressions could grow 10-50x, clicks likely at position 4

page_3.primary_query: how much house can i afford
page_3.why_no_click: 4 impressions at position 7.5 — very low volume. Either the query is niche or the page isn't ranking for the broader 'how much house can i afford' query.
page_3.competitor_weakness: Big sites have mortgage calculators but often lead-gen focused — QFINHUB's free/no-signup angle can differentiate
fix_plan: Improve title for broader 'how much house can i afford' query
Add interactive affordability calculator inline (not just link)
Add example: '$100k salary can afford ~$XXX house at 7%'
Add FAQ: 'How much house can I afford on my salary?'
Add internal links to mortgage-affordability calculator (money page)
page_3.expected_effect: Impressions increase if page matches broader query, clicks follow at position 7.5

note: These 3 pages are the best candidates based on available data. THE $20K LOAN BLOG AT POSITION 10.9 WITH 124 IMPRESSIONS IS THE #1 PRIORITY for first click.
